name: OTC pipeline + fontisan 0.4.9
body:
OpenType Collection partitioning, 95.93% Unicode 17 coverage

The canonical artifact is now an OpenType Collection partitioned across the 5 Unicode planes that carry assigned characters (BMP, SMP, SIP, TIP, SSP). Each subfont stays under the TrueType 65,535-glyph cap; the collection as a whole covers ~131,000 glyphs.

What's new

  • OTC subsystem — slim Ruby layer over Fontisan::Stitcher + Ucode::Unicode. No hand-rolled to_h/from_h; all (de)serialization goes through lutaml-model.
  • 10-face TTC — BMP sub-splits along CJK Unified Ideographs + Hangul Syllables boundaries so the post-composite glyph count never trips maxp.num_glyphs.
  • 138 donor families — bulk-added 108 Noto donors covering 127 Unicode blocks, plus FSung-2 for CJK Ext I + Compatibility Supplement, Noto Sans KR for Hangul Syllables (11172/11184), and 13 more Noto donors for partial-block gaps.
  • Synthetic SVG donors — 9 chart-only blocks (Gurung Khema, Khitan Small Script, Kirat Rai, Nyiakeng Puachue Hmong, Ottoman Siyaq Numbers, Sharada Supplement, Tangut Components Supplement, Tulu Tigalari, Tolong Siki) now ship real outlines extracted from Unicode code charts via SvgToGlyf.
  • 6 historic scripts wired — Noto Sans Modi, Cuneiform, Brahmi, Ugaritic, Avestan, Old Persian joined the manifest, closing 132 previously-tofu blocks on the website.
  • Rigorous coverage metric — denominator is now UCD-derived assigned codepoints (was range-size, which silently counted reserved/unassigned positions). Real number: 95.93%.
  • Release pipeline — single scripts/release.rb entry point emits OTC + per-plane TTFs + per-plane WOFF2/WOFF + coverage manifest + license pack + SVG exports + npm package + SRI hashes.

Upstream dependencies

  • fontisan 0.4.9 — first-class Stitcher remap, per-glyph unicode= attribute on SVG export, require "tmpdir" fix, BMP sub-split boundaries, lower partition cap.
  • ucode 0.3.0+ — canonical Unicode 17 plane/block catalog.

Known gaps

4 codepoints in Cuneiform are newly-assigned in Unicode 17 and not yet in Noto's release. Modi is at 79/96 (digits + punctuation pending in Noto). Brahmi is at 115/128 (3 combining marks absent).

Distribution formats

  • Essenfont-Regular.ttc — 94 MB, 10 faces, glyf outlines (canonical)
  • Essenfont-{BMP,SMP,SIP,TIP,SSP}.ttf — per-plane TTFs
  • Essenfont-{BMP,SMP,SIP,TIP,SSP}.woff2 — per-plane WOFF2 for web embed
  • coverage.json — per-block three-way classification (covered/uncovered/unassigned)

name: Initial alpha — donor registry + BMP build
body:
The donor registry ships

First tagged alpha. sources/manifest.yml declares the canonical Tier 1 OFL-licensed donor set: Noto Sans family, Full-Sung (Taiwan 全宋體), Lentariso, Kedebideri, UniHieroglyphica, NotoSerifTaiYo, Symbola, and ~120 more. The build assembles a BMP-only TTF from donor cmaps and produces per-block WOFF2 subsets.

Scope

  • BMP only — single TTF, ~63,000 assigned codepoints. SIP/TIP/SSP arrive in v0.2.0 with OTC partitioning.
  • Coverage ~62% of assigned Unicode 17 (PUA was polluting the metric; v0.2.0 fixes the denominator).
  • Coverage gate — build refuses any donor whose declared covers: block has zero cmap codepoints in the donor's actual cmap. Catches manifest typos at build time.
  • OFL 1.1 — donor license policy enforced: ofl_compatible allowlist plus FSung-NC accepted-with-conditions.

Known issues

  • Modi (U+11600) and Cuneiform (U+12132) are absent — no Noto donor in this release.
  • Latin-1 Supplement's U+0080..U+009F C1 controls are not in any donor.
  • CJK Ext G (TIP, U+30000) writes corrupted loca entries in the legacy TTF — fixed in v0.2.0 via OTC partitioning.
